summary: A bounded subprocess wrapper should be MetaBlooms' mandatory process-execution primitive. It must combine external subprocess hardening guidance with existing OS method reliability evidence and failure-learning policy.
synthesis_points: Native subprocess timeout is necessary but insufficient because process creation may not interrupt immediately; MetaBlooms must add receipt, total-budget, and failure-class enforcement.
Security posture should be deny-by-default: no shell, no command strings, no implicit PATH trust, no unbounded cwd/env/output.
Existing OS policy already identifies full archive recursive unzip validation as forbidden-by-default; wrapper must load it before any spawn.
The wrapper must record denials and failures as structured events so the method reliability router compounds over time.
